9ba6d152-d94d-45db-bd50-daa0f55cdbc4.name: Research/Plan
9ba6d152-d94d-45db-bd50-daa0f55cdbc4.prompt:
DO NOT USE TEMP DIRECTORIES. DO ALL WORK IN THIS DIRECTORY/SUBDIRECTORIES.

Read and thoroughly understand DESIGN_DOC.md and ARCHITECTURE.md. Today we are implementing Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, which splits DESIGN_DOC.md into multiple phases/waves. All waves before Wave $1 have alreayd been implemented -- read those only for informational purposes. Waves after Wave $1 are not relevant to us today, don't waste your time on them. Do not edit ANYTHING except ./plans/PLAN_{{turn}}.md and the contents of ./research

This is a RESEARCH AND PLANNING phase. Our task is to write a comprehensive ./plans/PLAN_{{turn}}.md that captures the user's vision from Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md but as a thoroughly researched and planned specification. The plan should NOT have a phase-by-phase structure. It should comprehensively include ALL of Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md from top to bottom, every single feature, nothing deferred or deprioritized, thoroughly. Make sure you understand Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md very carefully to a fine grain level.

As you construct your plan, you should do deep research on the best ways to execute Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md. A lot of this work has already been done in ARCHITECTURE.md, so don't repeat work that's already been done. Your research should include both a general search and a query of ./how-to-be-a-good-coder, an organized encyclopedia of important knowledge specific to various coding topics. See ./how-to-be-a-good-coder/ABOUT.md for details. Your research should include determining the best architectural choices, as well as the best technologies/tools for the job, so that you reinvent the wheel as little as possible. It should also include research into techniques for quickly bootstrapping projects so you aren't rewriting a bunch of boilerplate code. Your research can include scratch work and quick prototype coding in ./research so you can learn what might work or not work. 

Don't edit DESIGN_DOC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FULL_FEATURE_LIST.md, WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FULL_FEATURE_LIST.md, WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, ./audits, ./test-results, ./test-agents, ./how-to-be-a-good-coder

8b5b7a53-c097-430d-ad44-54fdac680e2a.name: Implement
8b5b7a53-c097-430d-ad44-54fdac680e2a.prompt:
DO NOT USE TEMP DIRECTORIES. DO ALL WORK IN THIS DIRECTORY/SUBDIRECTORIES.

Thoroughly follow the plan you created in ./plans/PLAN_{{turn}}.md. ./progress has all progress reports created so far. DO NOT OVERENGINEER. Do not incorporate fallbacks or failsafes; errors should never be handled silently. Examples of horrible fallbacks you should never do: checking if objects have attributes, forcibly trying to install packages mid-run, "safe" code branches when variables are unvavailable, etc. Stay consistent with architectural choices and paradigms made in the plan and the codebase. Comment and document as you write.

Don't edit DESIGN_DOC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FULL_FEATURE_LIST.md, WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, ./research, ./plans, ./audits, ./test-results, ./test-agents, ./how-to-be-a-good-coder

When you're done, answer the question: has every detail of ./plans/PLAN_{{turn}}.md been implemented with nothing deferred or deprioritized? Document the current state of the codebase in ./progress/PROGRESS_{{turn}}.{{subTurn}}.md

85a6caa9-f4da-4d89-941f-ef20bc6e4490.name: Audit
85a6caa9-f4da-4d89-941f-ef20bc6e4490.prompt:
DO NOT USE TEMP DIRECTORIES. DO ALL WORK IN THIS DIRECTORY/SUBDIRECTORIES.

Perform a comprehensive audit of the codebase against Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md. Create a full breakdown of everything in Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md that's missing from the codebase and store it in ./audits/AUDIT_{{turn}}.md. This should include every single detail, no matter how minor, with nothing deferred or deprioritized.

Don't edit DESIGN_DOC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FULL_FEATURE_LIST.md, WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, ./research, ./plans, ./test-results, ./test-agents, ./how-to-be-a-good-coder

Is anything missing?

e641e202-f1cc-4e6d-a8fb-5890fa762420.name: Create Tests
e641e202-f1cc-4e6d-a8fb-5890fa762420.prompt:
DO NOT USE TEMP DIRECTORIES. DO ALL WORK IN THIS DIRECTORY/SUBDIRECTORIES.

./test-agents contains a list of agents.md-style files that instruct an agent to simulate an end user that performs end-to-end testing on the codebase. Add five new test agent files. With each agent, you should be aiming to explore as much functionality at once of what's described in the Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md and all waves leading up to it. You should also be aiming to have each agent act differently from each other (which requires cross-referencing against the current documents in ./test-agents). If there are very few agents so far, you should be creating agents that test core functionality and simulate realistic users. If there are many agents so far, you should be creating agents that explore edge case behavior.

While building these test agents, you can reference ./how-to-be-a-good-coder, an organized encyclopedia of important knowledge specific to various coding topics. See ./how-to-be-a-good-coder/ABOUT.md for details.

Don't deploy the test agents yet. Don't edit DESIGN_DOC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FULL_FEATURE_LIST.md, WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, ./research, ./plans, ./test-results, ./how-to-be-a-good-coder

Answer this question when you're finished: will more test agents be required beyond the ones created so far for fully testing all functionality in Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md (from top to bottom without deferring or deprioritizng any features)?

c2ef89fc-81df-4f8d-8266-6d8d00cd3f8a.name: Research/Plan Audit
c2ef89fc-81df-4f8d-8266-6d8d00cd3f8a.prompt:
DO NOT USE TEMP DIRECTORIES. DO ALL WORK IN THIS DIRECTORY/SUBDIRECTORIES.

Read and thoroughly understand DESIGN_DOC.md and ARCHITECTURE.md. Today we are implementing Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, which splits DESIGN_DOC.md into multiple phases/waves. All waves before Wave $1 have already been implemented -- read those only for informational purposes. Waves after Wave $1 are not relevant to us today, don't waste your time on them. Do not edit ANYTHING except ./plans/PLAN_{{turn}}.md and the contents of ./research

We have a partial implementation of Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md already, but it failed the audit. Read and understand ./audits/AUDIT_{{prevTurn}}.md. Our goal is to create a full implementation of Wave $1 of WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md by augmenting the current codebase based on the audit, being careful to preserve good architectural/technological/testing choices.

This is a RESEARCH AND PLANNING phase. Our task is to write a comprehensive ./plans/PLAN_{{turn}}.md. The plan should NOT have a phase-by-phase structure. It should comprehensively include ALL concerns laid out in ./audits/AUDIT_{{prevTurn}}.md from top to bottom, every single issue, nothing deferred or deprioritized, thoroughly. As you construct your plan, you should do research on the best ways to address the concerns in the audit. A lot of this work has already been done in ARCHITECTURE.md, so don't repeat work that's already been done. Your research should include both a general search and a query of ./how-to-be-a-good-coder, an organized encyclopedia of important knowledge specific to various coding topics. See ./how-to-be-a-good-coder/ABOUT.md for details. Your research should include determining the best architectural choices, as well as the best technologies/tools for the job, so that you reinvent the wheel as little as possible. It should also include research into techniques for quickly bootstrapping projects so you aren't rewriting a bunch of boilerplate code. Your research can include scratch work and quick prototype coding in ./research so you can learn what might work or not work. 

If the existing research in ./research addresses all concerns and the existing architecture is sound, this research/planning phase should not take very long.

Don't edit DESIGN_DOC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FULL_FEATURE_LIST.md, WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, ./audits, ./test-results, ./test-agents, ./how-to-be-a-good-coder

3603ca83-c8b8-44d9-9bc4-e86f69c64fc1.name: Run Tests
3603ca83-c8b8-44d9-9bc4-e86f69c64fc1.prompt:
DO NOT USE TEMP DIRECTORIES. DO ALL WORK IN THIS DIRECTORY/SUBDIRECTORIES.

For each file in ./test-agents, deploy an agent treating that file as an agents.md file. This agent will perform e2e testing on the codebase based on the behaviors prescribed in the .md file. Direct each agent to write results to its own uniquely identifiable output file in the subdirectory ./test_results/TEST_RESULTS_{{turn}}

Don't edit DESIGN_DOC.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FULL_FEATURE_LIST.md, WAVE_BREAKDOWN.md, ./research, ./plans, ./test-agents, ./how-to-be-a-good-coder
